Slide 4: The art and science of prompting

The art and science of prompting

Prompts are how you ask Copilot Chat to do something for you — like creating, summarizing, reviewing, or researching.

Think about prompting like having a conversation, using plain but clear language and providing context like you would with an assistant.

Tell Copilot Chat what you need

Review text: "Check this product launch rationale for inconsistencies."

Summarize information: "Write a session abstract of this [presentation]."

Create engaging content: "Create a value proposition for our new product using [Product Info]."

Research what’s happening: “Give me the latest industry news on Generational AI"

Slide 5: The Art and Science of prompting

The Art and Science of prompting

To get the best response, it’s important to focus on some of the key elements when phrasing your Copilot prompts.

Include the right prompt ingredients

Goal What response do you want from Copilot Chat? Context Why do you need it and who is involved? Source Which information sources or samples should Copilot Chat use? Expectations How should Copilot Chat respond to best meet your expectations?

Slide 6

Goal What response do you want from Copilot Chat? Context Why do you need it and who is involved? Which information sources or samples should Copilot Chat use? Source How should Copilot Chat respond to best meet your expectations? Expectations Help me write an email to inform the customer about a product change. Since the customer is not a global administrator, I need to reference the URL that describes the change, and provide clear and concise information. Include the right prompt ingredients

Slide 7

Art of Prompting

Elements of a Great Prompt
InstructionTell me about this or do that
Rolean identity that the AI is expected to emulate, act like, or duplicate in it’s reply – “act like a…”
Contextshow the AI more info about where/what it is – could be data, examples, previous articles, etc. “What are video codecs?” vs. “Can you create an outline for a 20 minute tutorial to teach support engineers about video codecs?”
Tone/Style Informationtone of writing – serious, academic, empathetic, inspiring, can be “write like this examples”
Formatting Informationtable, markup language, code, etc.
An Example or Examplesgive it data to show what you want – the more the better – above 20 not helpful

Copilot in Teams During Meetings

Microsoft Teams Logo, symbol, meaning, history, PNG

Copilot in Teams Meeting only Mode..

Copilot After-Meetings

How to use: Copilot in Teams for chat

Sample use cases

  • Catch up on a chat thread with a summary or by getting key points and action items
  • Draft a new chat entry in the thread

In the Teams chat window

  • Rewrite a draft and adjust for length and tone

Use the Copilot chat pane to

  • Ask questions about the content of the chat – does not work in Channels
  • Summarize over a period of time
  • Ask about decisions, open items, tasks
  • Ask what a specific person said
  • Ask about links
Copilot Chat Entry and Prompt Guide Instructions
ActionResult
When creating a chat entry click on the Copilot icon- (opens in new window)---to show the Rewrite and Adjust prompts when drafting a new chat
Click on the Prompt Guide icon____ to show the prompts to edit a slide or learn about the presentation

How to use: Copilot in Teams during a meeting

Note the specific prompts shown may vary

  • Get help during a meeting with a summary, key points, sentiment, or potential questions
  • Catch up on a meeting with a summary or by asking questions
  • Works during 1:1 and group calls, Meet Now, Scheduled private meetings, recurring meetings, Channel meetings

Use the Copilot chat pane to

  • Get a summary of the meeting so far
  • Key discussion points
  • Who said what or what people said
  • Where do people disagree
  • Suggest action items
Prompt Guide Icon Usage during Meetings
ActionResult
Click on the Prompt Guide icon__ to show list of curated prompts

How to use: Copilot in Teams after a meeting

Note the specific prompts shown may vary

  • Catch up on a meeting with a summary or by asking questions
  • Works for scheduled meetings with a transcript (1:1 and group)

Use the Copilot chat pane on the Recap tab to

  • Recap the meeting
  • List action items
  • Follow up questions
  • Highlight the different perspectives on a topic
  • List main ideas

Click on the Prompt Guide icon ____ to show the prompts to ask questions about the meeting content

What Happens to recordings and Transcripts

For Non-Channel (Standard Teams Meeting)

  • Stored in OneDrive of Person who clicked Record
  • OneDrive→ Recordings

For Channel Teams Meeting

  • Teams Channel->Files-> recordings

Now Lets take a look at few more examples…

  • Recap Meetings
  • Track Action Items Automatically
  • Be Specific
  • Plan Agendas Without the Hassle
  • Search Smarter, Not Harder

Recap meetings

Use Copilot to Summarize key takeaways, track actions, and highlight decisions from any recorded meetings

Prompt: “Summarize the last meeting and list all actions items assigned to me.”

Recap meetings (Continued 1)

Recap meetings

Recap meetings (Continued 2)

Recap meetings (Continued)

Track Action Items

Use Copilot to extract action items from meetings, chats, and posts- so we don’t miss a thing.

Prompt: “What action items were assigned to me this week in this meeting /.”

Track Action items - Summarize

Track Action items

Summarize my last project meeting / and list action items

Track Action items - Draft Agenda

Create a draft agenda for next [Meeting]based on previous discussion

Track Action items - Follow-up Email

Write a follow-up email to attendees summarizing today’s discussion and next steps

Track Action items - Decisions

What Decisions were made in the last meeting /

Be Specific

When Using Copilot in Teams, specificity helps. It helps Copilot Understand your Intent and generate more relevant content

Prompt: “What did [person] say about the topic []

What did [person] say about [topic]? in Copilot in Teams

  • Try this prompt in your next meeting What did [person] say about [x]?

Summarize all emails, Teams chats, and meetings involving [Manager’s Name] this week. Highlight key decisions, pending actions, and deadlines.

Post Meeting agenda planning in Copilot in Teams

Build an agenda for a follow up meeting based on this call. Include reference in the agenda to the conversation thread with brief summary.

Getting Specific ... in Copilot in Teams

  • Try this prompt in your next meeting What were some concerns raised by / about "" ? Provide more details on the use cases for [ topic] that [user] explained

Post Meeting agenda planning in Copilot in Teams

Build an agenda for a follow up meeting based on this call. Include reference in the agenda to the conversation thread with brief summary.

Search Smarter not Harder

Traditional search is Outdated

Copilot Search is Context-Aware and Conversational

Where is the location of the deck [david] created for all presentations?

Could you help me locate the conversation where [Megan] shared the link to the NC Innovation Day Registration Excel file?

What did [ person] say about [Topic] on this [meeting] who is covering it

Chat and Channel Summaries

What are Open Items ? Summarize what I've missed

Highlights from the Past 7 Days

Highlights from this conversation

What were some suggested Workarounds

Identify sentiment or concerns in Copilot in Teams

  • Try this prompt in your next meeting

List the biggest concern for each participant in the meeting.

Are there any unresolved items or any outstanding open items that need addressing?

Suggest follow-up questions

To Sum Things Up

  • Try this prompt in your next meeting

Summarize key takeaways, decisions, and action items from today’s meeting, identify unresolved topics, and draft a proposed agenda for our next session—then prepare a follow-up message I can send to the team

New Feature (forthcoming! Not yet in GCC)

Facilitator is a Teams agent that will automatically generate notes, manage meeting time with updates, summarize meeting decisions, create tasks, and much more. Use Facilitator tin the meeting chat to ask meeting questions or external question that require the Internet. Start Facilitator when you enter your meeting, or schedule it to start automatically when scheduling a meeting. when you call ends, let Facilitator wrap everything up into a neat Loop component for easy access for all meeting participants.

Start Facilitator and notes

Step 1: Select More actions Step 2: Select Turn on Facilitator

A: Facilitator will automatically start taking notes B: Facilitator will automatically track tasks

Tips & Tricks

Use Copilot during meetings:

Don’t wait until after Summarize the discussion so far

Getting real-time info

Highlight a pending question no one answered yet What decisions were made in this chat?

  • Use tables and lists: Ask for a table (options, pros/cons) as mentioned, helps to digest complex discussions

List the questions asked during this meeting & the person that asked the question & who responded. List any open items that have not been addressed in this meeting. Output in table format.

Prompt Gallery inspiration

Copilot Chat (both M365 + CP Chat) includes a Prompt Gallery

Find more Teams prompts to try in Copilot Lab

Find action items

Are there any action items for me?

Compare Ideas

For each idea discussed, identify the pros and cons formatted as a table with 3 headers: idea, pros, cons.

What are the options?

Create a table of the options discussed with pros and cons

List ideas

List 5 ideas discussed

Get the schedule

List the key dates in a table

Get clarity

List the different opinions and suggest clarifying questions to ask for each

What decisions were made

Did the team achieve consensus on a decision and what was it?

Be prepared

What are the goals and topics from the meeting? Format each section with a bolded heading, and bolded names

Keep meetings moving

What questions can the group ask to generate more ideas or insights? Limit each to 30-60 characters

Summarize meetings

Summarize what was discussed about
